intro: Pithecellobium tubuliferum, also known as Monkey's Ears or Monkey Tail, is a tropical legume tree native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation, treating fever, and aiding in the management of respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Pithecellobium tubuliferum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects.
